subject: Computer Networking
title: From URL to Rendered Page
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on From URL to Rendered Page.

hook.text: Type a URL, press Enter, and a page appears. Behind that moment are dozens of steps in milliseconds.

buildup.text: The browser resolves DNS, opens a TCP connection, performs a TLS handshake, then sends the HTTP request.

discovery.text: The server responds with HTML. The browser parses it into a DOM tree, then fetches CSS, JS, and images.

twist.text: CSS blocks rendering until it's parsed. JS blocks parsing unless marked async or defer. Order matters hugely.

climax.text: The render tree combines DOM and CSSOM. The browser calculates layout, paints pixels, and composites layers.

punchline.text: URL to pixels in under a second. A symphony of protocols.

quiz.question: What blocks HTML parsing in the browser by default?
options: JavaScript that is not marked async or defer
Image files referenced in the HTML
